hero.title:
Where you can say "I'm home" —
that's where family begins.
hero.sub:
Photo weddings in Shonan, Yokohama, Komagane & Toyohashi
Free consultation / Over 1,000 couples photographed

stats.label4:
Satisfaction rate
"So glad we chose them"

brand.mission:
Where the land speaks, photos endure,
and weddings become the "kotosio" of a lifetime.
brand.value1_desc:
Because you met by that sea.
Because you grew up near those mountains.
When there's a reason, photos carry meaning.
brand.value2_desc:
You don't need a grand ceremony.
Photos alone are enough.
What's right is what feels right for you.
brand.value3_desc:
Someday, when you show your children,
you'll say, "This is where it all began" —
that's the kind of photo we create.

region.title: Every place has a reason.
region.text:
Because you met by that sea.
Because you grew up near those mountains.
Because that's where the proposal happened.

"Why here?"
That answer gives depth to every photograph.

yokohama.name: Yokohama
yokohama.tagline: The harbor, the light, and the time you walked together
yokohama.feature1_desc: From iconic spots to hidden gems — your favorite places
yokohama.feature2_desc: Port lights and silhouettes of the two of you
yokohama.feature3_desc: We can arrange a special dinner after the shoot
yokohama.story_title: The harbor, the light, and the time you walked together.
yokohama.story_text:
The Minato Mirai skyline, the red brick streets.
In the place where your moments overlap, a new story begins.
yokohama.quote:
The time you spent in Yokohama
becomes the history of you two.

shonan.name: Shonan
shonan.tagline: Sea and light, the landscape of your roots
shonan.feature1_desc: Open, liberating shoots with the ocean as your backdrop
shonan.feature2_desc: Timeless charm in the old streets of Kamakura
shonan.feature3_desc: Bathed in sunset — a moment of pure bliss
shonan.gallery_alt1: Shonan photo wedding — railroad crossing by the sea
shonan.gallery_alt2: Shonan photo wedding — couple on the beach
shonan.gallery_alt3: Shonan photo wedding — traditional kimono in Kamakura
shonan.gallery_alt4: Shonan photo wedding — traditional kimono in Chigasaki
shonan.gallery_alt5: Shonan photo wedding — couple with the Enoden train
shonan.story_title: Sea and light, the landscape of your roots.
shonan.story_text:
The silhouette of Enoshima, the hidden lanes of Kamakura.
At the sea you hold dear, a new view of the future.
shonan.quote:
Carried by the ocean breeze —
the landscape where you began.

komagane.name: Komagane
komagane.tagline: Watched over by the Southern Alps — a forest where family is born
komagane.feature1_desc: Parents, grandparents, relatives — everyone is welcome
komagane.feature2_desc: Kozenji Temple, forest parks, pastoral scenery
komagane.feature3_desc: Combine a family trip with your photo session
komagane.story_title:
Watched over by the Southern Alps —
a forest where family is born.
komagane.story_text:
The landscape you grew up in, the place you return to.
Surrounded by mountains, a new family begins its journey.
komagane.quote:
Embraced by mountains,
a new family is born.

toyohashi.name: Toyohashi
toyohashi.tagline: A warm town where people connect
toyohashi.feature2_desc: Shoots near your family home — just ask
toyohashi.feature3_desc: Hometown family sessions, unique to here
toyohashi.gallery_alt1: Toyohashi photo wedding — sunset on the rocks
toyohashi.gallery_alt4: Toyohashi photo wedding — autumn leaves & kimono
toyohashi.gallery_alt5: Toyohashi photo wedding — couple on the rocks
toyohashi.story_text:
Yoshida Castle ruins, the warmth of the shopping streets.
Wrapped in the kindness of this town, a family's new chapter begins.
toyohashi.quote:
The warmth of the town
wraps around the two of you.

testimonials.title: What Our Couples Say
testimonials.quote1: "I grew up in Shonan, so I really wanted photos by the sea. We invited my parents too. When my mom cried after the shoot, I knew we made the right choice. We didn't need a big ceremony — this was perfect."
testimonials.quote2: "It was a second marriage, and we had kids. I was a little nervous at first, but they told us not to worry at all. We got family photos with the children, and it really felt like 'a new family.'"
testimonials.quote3: "We invited both families and turned it into a trip. They handled the hotel and dinner — everything as a package — so it was easy. The photos are great, but honestly, the trip itself is the memory."
testimonials.quote4: "We'd decided not to have a ceremony. But we still wanted photos. I didn't want anything cheap-looking, so we chose here — and it was the right call. A quiet moment for just the two of us was exactly what we needed."

painpoints.title: Sound familiar?
painpoints.q1: "I want photos in my hometown. I want my parents to see."
painpoints.a1: Where you were born, where you met, where the proposal happened. Photos taken in that place carry a meaning nothing else can. The Shonan sea, the Yokohama harbor, the Komagane mountains, the streets of Toyohashi. The place where you can say "I'm home."
painpoints.q2: "It's a remarriage, so I'd like to keep it low-key."
painpoints.a2: Let's give shape to your "this time, for real" feelings. You can include your children in the shoot. No need to worry about what others think — enjoy a quiet, intimate moment for just the two of you. A fresh starting line for your new family.
painpoints.q3: "More than a ceremony, I want time with family."
painpoints.a3: Accommodation, dining, photography, local experiences — all in one package. Like a family trip where both families come together. We'll plan everything, from the shoot to the dinner afterwards.
painpoints.q4: "We're not having a ceremony, but we want photos."
painpoints.a4: That's a perfectly good choice. Photos alone can tell your story beautifully. Not a cheap shoot — but the kind of photo that, years from now, still makes your heart swell.

comparison.title: What Makes Us Different
comparison.note:
We're not selling a location.
We're preserving your story, in the place it belongs. That's it.

service.title: Services
service.desc1: A quiet, intimate moment for just the two of you. Family can join too. Kimono, dress — your choice. Location, style — all up to you.
service.desc2: Photos plus a small ceremony. Invite your family, share a meal together. Relaxed yet memorable.
service.desc3: More than a ceremony. Stay, dine, explore. A full family trip wrapped around your special day.
service.desc4: If nothing fits, we'll create it. Tailored to your life, built from zero.

flow.title: How It Works
flow.step1_desc: Reach out via LINE or our form. "I'm kind of thinking about it" is totally fine. No pressure.
flow.step2_desc: Location, outfit, timing, guest count — share your rough ideas and we'll take it from there.
flow.step3_desc: Our photographer and crew will be with you — just enjoy it. Even if you're nervous, it'll all come together.
flow.step4_desc: All photo data is yours. Photo books and albums available too.

faq.title: FAQ
faq.q1: Do we need to bring our own outfits?
faq.q2: Can our parents and relatives join the shoot?
faq.q3: Is it OK if we're remarrying or have children?
faq.q5: How far in advance should we book?
faq.q6: Can you arrange accommodation and meals?
faq.q7: Do we get all the photo data?
faq.a1: No need. We can provide wedding dresses, tuxedos, traditional kimono, or casual wear — and you can mix and match. "Start in kimono, change into a dress" is totally doable. Children's outfits available too — just ask.
faq.a2: Absolutely — in fact, we encourage it. We've done three-generation shoots many times. Grandparents, parents, siblings, relatives. Family photos only grow more precious over time.
faq.a3: Of course. We're here to help you shape your "this time, for real" moment. Shoot with your children, or have a quiet moment for just the two of you. Whatever your situation, your starting line is right here.
faq.a4: We'll reschedule — no cancellation fee. That said, rainy-day shoots have their own charm. Walking under an umbrella, listening to the rain. Those photos become unexpectedly meaningful.
faq.a5: About 2–3 months ahead is a good guide. Spring and autumn fill up fast, so the earlier the better. But "how about next week?" works too. Just reach out.
faq.a6: We know the best local stays and restaurants in each area. Let's plan not just the shoot, but the time around it. We can help with the whole trip.
faq.a7: Yes — currently we hand over everything, no extra charge. Take home every captured moment. If you'd like prints or a photo book, we can discuss that separately.
faq.a8: Photos only: from ¥200,000. Photos + small ceremony: from ¥400,000. Family trip: from ¥1,000,000. Start with a free consultation and let's figure out "roughly something like this" together.

cta.title: Start with a Free Consultation
cta.text:
Curious about how it works? Reach out anytime.
LINE, phone, or our form — whichever suits you best.

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.intro:
Let's start by talking.
No need to overthink it.
"What's it like?" is all you need to say.
contact.label_message: Questions or anything you'd like to share *
contact.note:
※ We'll reply within 2 business days.
※ Your information will only be used for this consultation.
contact.placeholder_message:
Anything at all is fine.

Example: We met in Shonan, so we'd love to shoot there
Example: We have children — is that OK?
Example: I'd like a rough idea of the cost

about.title: About Us
about.intro:
KOTOSIO provides photo weddings and location shoots
across four special regions: Shonan, Yokohama, Komagane, and Toyohashi.
Since 2004, we've been there for the moment
when family begins — in a place you can call home.
about.loc1_desc: Sun-drenched waves of Shonan. Open beaches and the quiet beauty of ancient Kamakura. In this place filled with summer memories, your story unfolds.
about.loc2_desc: Harbor breezes and sunset vistas. The Red Brick Warehouse, Yamashita Park. Where city and sea harmonize, creating refined and timeless photos.
about.loc3_desc: Nestled between the Southern and Central Alps — forests, villages, and four seasons of wonder. A wedding watched over by mountains and sky.
about.loc4_desc: Gentle waves of Mikawa Bay and pastoral countryside. Where history and nature coexist, capturing warm family moments in every frame.
about.philosophy_title:
Where the land speaks, photos endure,
and weddings become the "kotosio" of a lifetime.
about.philosophy_text:
We don't sell locations.
We help preserve the story you share with that place, in the form of photographs.
Someday, when you show your children and grandchildren, you'll say
"This is where it all began" — with pride.

exit_popup.title:
Still thinking it over?
Let's just talk.
exit_popup.text:
It's free, no strings attached.
"What's it like?" is all you need to say.
Reach out via LINE or our form.
